perturbation.original_text: 5.5 Insurance During the term of this Agreement, you shall maintain in force under policies of insurance issued by licensed insurers approved by us insurance coverage as we from time to time require. You must maintain insurance related to your operation of the Franchised Business. Such insurance coverage will include:
perturbation.changed_text: 5.5 Insurance During the term of this Agreement, you should endeavor to maintain insurance under policies of insurance issued by licensed insurers. You should try to maintain insurance related to your operation of the Franchised Business. Such insurance coverage could include:
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates specific insurance coverage. The revised text uses 'should endeavor' and 'should try,' making the insurance requirement discretionary rather than mandatory. Also, replacing "will include" with "could include" weakens the obligation to maintain specific insurance types, creating ambiguity.
perturbation.law_explanation: NY Ins. Law § 2103 requires specific insurance policies to be maintained. The modified clause introduces ambiguity, making it unclear if any insurance is actually required. This contradicts the intent and legal framework established by New York insurance laws which expect clarity on obligations.

2025-03-28 2025-05-16 Share Facebook Email SECTION 2103 Insurance agents; licensing Insurance (ISC) CHAPTER 28, ARTICLE 21 § 2103. Insurance agents; licensing. (a) The superintendent may issue a license to any person, firm or corporation who or which has complied with the requirements of this chapter, authorizing such licensee to act as an insurance agent with respect to the lines of authority for life insurance, variable life and variable annuity products, or accident and health insurance and sickness or any other line of authority deemed to be similar by the superintendent, including for this purpose, health maintenance organization contracts, legal services insurance or with respect to any combination of the above, as specified in such license, on behalf of any insurer, fraternal benefit society or health maintenance organization, which is authorized to do such kind or kinds of insurance or health maintenance organization business in this state. (b) The superintendent may issue a license to any person, firm, association or corporation who or which has complied with the requirements of this chapter, authorizing the licensee to act as agent of any authorized insurer, other than an insurer specified in subsection (b) of section two thousand one hundred fifteen of this article, with respect to the lines of authority for accident and health or sickness, property, casualty, personal lines or any other line of authority granted other than life, and variable life and variable annuity products, which such insurer is authorized to do in this state. (c) Any such license issued to a firm or association shall authorize only the members thereof, named in such license as sub-licensees, to act individually as agents thereunder, and any such license issued to a corporation shall authorize only the officers and directors thereof, named in such license as sub-licensees, to act individually as agents thereunder. Every sub-licensee, acting as insurance agent pursuant to such a license shall be authorized so to act only in the name of the
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates insurance coverage, whereas the changed text uses the terms 'should endeavor' and 'should try', which makes the insurance requirement discretionary. Furthermore, the language 'will include' has been changed to 'could include,' further weakening the obligation to maintain specific insurance types. The Perturbation explanation confirms that these modifications make the insurance requirement optional. The Law explanation states that NY Ins. Law § 2103 requires specific insurance policies to be maintained. The revised text contradicts the law by creating ambiguity around the mandatory insurance requirements, suggesting compliance is not strictly required which directly opposes the law's intent.

perturbation.original_text: 5.7 Payment of Taxes You shall pay all personal property, sales, excise, use, and other taxes, regardless of type or nature, which may be imposed, levied, assessed or charged, on, against, or in connection with the Franchised Business and any products, services or equipment sold or furnished hereunder, whether those taxes are imposed by any federal, state, municipality, county or parish, or other governmental unity or agency, which may have jurisdiction over such products, services and equipment. It shall be your sole responsibility to insure that any Unit Franchisee operating in the Master Territory shall also comply with this Section 5.7 as it may apply to the operation of the Unit Franchisee's business.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.7 Payment of Taxes You should consider paying most personal property, sales, excise, use, and other taxes, regardless of type or nature, which may be imposed. It could be your responsibility to encourage that any Unit Franchisee operating in the Master Territory should also attempt to comply with this Section 5.7.
perturbation.explanation: The original text contains a clear legal obligation to pay all taxes. The modified text replaces 'shall pay' with 'should consider paying,' making tax payment discretionary. Also, changing 'It shall be your sole responsibility to insure that...' to 'It could be your responsibility to encourage that...' significantly weakens the obligation to ensure Unit Franchisees comply with tax laws.
perturbation.law_explanation: NY Tax Law § 1131 mandates the payment of sales tax. By making the payment of taxes discretionary with the use of "should consider", the modified clause creates ambiguity that directly opposes the mandatory requirements in New York State tax law.

2025-05-30 2025-06-06 Share Facebook Email SECTION 1131 Definitions Tax (TAX) CHAPTER 60, ARTICLE 28, PART 4 § 1131. Definitions. When used in this part IV, (1) "Persons required to collect tax" or "person required to collect any tax imposed by this article" shall include: every vendor of tangible personal property or services; every recipient of amusement charges; every operator of a hotel or short term rental unit; every booking service with respect to the rent for every occupancy of a short term rental unit it facilitates as described in paragraph ten of subdivision (c) of section eleven hundred one of this article; and every marketplace provider with respect to sales of tangible personal property it facilitates as described in paragraph one of subdivision (e) of section eleven hundred one of this article. Said terms shall also include any officer, director or employee of a corporation or of a dissolved corporation, any employee of a partnership, any employee or manager of a limited liability company, or any employee of an individual proprietorship who as such officer, director, employee or manager is under a duty to act for such corporation, partnership, limited liability company or individual proprietorship in complying with any requirement of this article, or has so acted; and any member of a partnership or limited liability company. Provided, however, that any person who is a vendor solely by reason of clause (D) or (E) of subparagraph (i) of paragraph eight of subdivision (b) of section eleven hundred one of this article shall not be a "person required to collect any tax imposed by this article" until twenty days after the date by which such person is required to file a certificate of registration pursuant to section eleven hundred thirty-four of this part. Such terms shall not include an operator of a short term rental unit who rents out the operator's own property for three days or fewer in a calendar year and does not use a booking service to facilitate such rental. (2) "Customer" shall include: every purchas
perturbation.justification: The modified text creates a contradiction by changing the mandatory 'shall pay' to the discretionary 'should consider paying' regarding tax obligations. The original text imposes a clear duty to ensure franchisee compliance, which is weakened in the modified text. The Law Explanation states that NY Tax Law § 1131 mandates the payment of sales tax. Scraped Law Snippet 1 defines 'persons required to collect tax,' indicating a legal obligation to collect and remit taxes, which the modified text undermines by making tax payment discretionary. The original text creates a clear legal obligation while the modified text creates ambiguity.

perturbation.original_text: 5.14 Solicitation Advertising You shall conduct advertising to solicit Applicants for the purchase of Unit Franchisees in the Master Territory ("Solicitation Advertising"). You shall expend not less than $5,000 each month on such Solicitation Advertising.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.14 Solicitation Advertising You may want to conduct some advertising to solicit Applicants for the purchase of Unit Franchisees in the Master Territory ("Solicitation Advertising"). You might expend around $5,000 each month on such Solicitation Advertising.
perturbation.explanation: The original text imposes a clear obligation to conduct advertising and spend a minimum amount. The modified text replaces 'shall conduct' with 'may want to conduct,' making advertising discretionary. The term 'shall expend not less than' is replaced with 'might expend around', introducing uncertainty and allowing for spending less than $5,000 or nothing at all.
perturbation.law_explanation: 15 U.S. Code § 41 grants the FTC power to prevent unfair methods of competition in commerce. If the master franchise agreement sets a minimum marketing spend to properly market the unit franchises, changing 'shall' to 'may want' could be interpreted as an attempt to weaken the franchisee's ability to effectively compete, potentially violating FTC regulations against unfair competition.

perturbation.justification: The original contract mandates advertising and a minimum spend, while the changed version makes both discretionary. The Law Explanation states that changing 'shall' to 'may want' could weaken the franchisee's ability to compete, violating FTC regulations. Scraped Law Snippet 1 mentions 15 USC Ch. 2, concerning the 'PREVENTION OF UNFAIR METHODS OF COMPETITION', and Snippet 2 references the 'Federal Trade Commission Act' which authorizes 15 USC 41, reinforcing the FTC's power to prevent unfair competition. By removing the mandatory nature of advertising and minimum spending, the changed text creates an ambiguity that could potentially hinder fair competition, establishing a contradiction with the FTC's mandate.
